literal: To apply physical or direct control or restraint on something or someone.

relabelled_senses.definition: To see or catch sight of someone or something.
A contest or match in a stick-and-ball field sport in which players use ash-wood sticks, especially hurling or shinty; used as a name or headline for such fixtures.

literal: A student who acts as a joker or clown to amuse others.

relabelled_senses.definition: Free from guilt, blame, or wrongdoing; morally blameless and not implicated in improper conduct.
Money obtained by lawful, legitimate means and not derived from criminal activity.
To beat someone severely, typically in a physical fight.
To improve one's behavior, performance, or conduct; to reform oneself in order to meet expected standards.
To record one's arrival time at a place of employment using a time clock or electronic attendance system.
Almost successful or nearly correct, but falling short of the desired result.
Something or someone that is mysterious, unknown, or not easily understood; not open to inquiry or comprehension.
Sufficiently accurate, adequate, or acceptable for practical purposes despite not being perfect or exactly precise.
To move closer to a person, place, or target, especially so as to surround, capture, or corner them.
To shut the eyelids; to stop seeing for a time, as to rest or sleep; used euphemistically to refer to death.
A jocular or euphemistic instruction, historically given to someone (traditionally a woman) to endure an unpleasant or compromising physical act by imagining something proper or respectable.
To become unwilling or resistant to consider new ideas, information, or arguments; to adopt a closed-minded attitude.
To follow closely after in time or sequence; to occur immediately subsequent to something else.
To bring an investigation, dispute, or matter to an end; to consider it settled and cease further action or discussion.
To finalize and record all financial transactions for a defined accounting period so that the accounts are complete and ready for audit or reporting.
To take precautions or remedial action after the damage has already occurred, rendering the effort pointless or ineffective; to act too late.
